title: rosa × dumetorum var. fastigiata
intro: Rosa × dumetorum var. fastigiata, also known as Rosa fastigiata, is a hybrid of the Rosa dumetorum and Rosa gallica, and is a type of flowering shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Rosa fastigiata include its ability to be used as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant agent, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and fever. The health benefits of Rosa fastigiata are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-cancer properties, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. The herbal preparations of Rosa fastigiata typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from its flowers and leaves, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, including colds, fever, and digestive issues.
